author: developeruche
content:
All these are ready and the numbers are great!

Specs: https://hackmd.io/@0xdeveloperuche/SkScRpTObe


RETH + EXEX server over a custom TCP: https://hackmd.io/nz8dncoPRY6btL_uOQmWpQ#Lighthouse--RETH-using-EXEX-for-witness-indexing-served-over-TCP


RETH + EXEX server over a custom TCP, using `sendfile` not `DB`: https://hackmd.io/nz8dncoPRY6btL_uOQmWpQ#Lighthouse--RETH-using-EXEX-for-witness-indexing-served-over-TCP-using-sendfile-rather-than-DB-reads

author: taulepton_
content: Great work on this, it looks very promising! The key takeaway is that we can achieve a 2 orders-of-magnitude latency improvement by moving away from JSON-RPC.

author: taulepton_
content: <@944007601400741989> what are your thoughts about custom TCP vs HTTP? I'm wondering if maybe the UX of having custom TCP would make it less adaptable / natural with pre-existing libraries frameworks or do you think thats not a problem?

author: taulepton_
content: cc: <@427491045308235776> see above for latency improvmeents regarding migrating away from json-rpc for witness fetching.

author: developeruche
content:
The UX for custom TCP is fairly different from the traditional HTTP, but not complicated… the Rust and Typescript implementation is very straightforward.

Building on already existing libraries from ‘Tokio’ or ‘std’ for rust and ‘net’ for typescript the custom TCP implementation can be done with as little as 30 LOC

author: taulepton_
content: Makes sense. Can you open a PR to implement this in the reth-wintess-indexer repo please? It would aslo be great if you can create a new crate for the TCP client so we can import that client in downstream repos and make requests against the node using it.
